name: Phoenix (United States)
description: A maroon field charged at the centre with a stylised white phoenix bird.
history: Adopted in 1990 following a city-wide design competition, with the winning design by graphic designer John Yeagley.

features.symbolism: A maroon (burgundy) field referencing the desert and the city's southwestern setting.

A stylised phoenix — the mythical bird that rises reborn from its own ashes — referring directly to the city's name and to its rise on the site of the ancient Hohokam civilisation. The design evokes both a phoenix in flight and a sun.
